comment_text.sn7.13:1.2: Vātehābādhika is a syntactic compound, “afflicted by winds” (vātehi). It is one of the illnesses noted at AN 10.60:7.4. In Ayurveda, diseases of the winds may have many causes, chief among them faulty digestion.
comment_text.sn7.13:1.3: In DN 16:5.4.5 Upavāna was said to have been the Buddha’s carer for a long time. At DN 29:41.1 he is fanning the Buddha. At AN 5.166, Ānanda goes to Upavāṇa for support when he feels he has disappointed the Buddha. The events of this discourse are recalled by him in his verses at Thag 2.33.
comment_text.sn7.13:1.5: Typically ghee or other oil is recommended rather than hot water.

root_text.sn7.13:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā vātehābādhiko hoti;
root_text.sn7.13:1.3: āyasmā ca upavāṇo bhagavato upaṭṭhāko hoti.
root_text.sn7.13:1.4: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ upavāṇaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn7.13:1.6: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā upavāṇo bhagavato paṭissutvā nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena devahitassa brāhmaṇassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā tuṇhībhūto ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn7.13:1.7: Addasā kho devahito brāhmaṇo āyasmantaṁ upavāṇaṁ tuṇhībhūtaṁ ekamantaṁ ṭhitaṁ.
root_text.sn7.13:5.1: Atha kho devahito brāhmaṇo uṇhodakassa kājaṁ purisena gāhāpetvā phāṇitassa ca puṭaṁ āyasmato upavāṇassa pādāsi.
root_text.sn7.13:5.2: Atha kho āyasmā upavāṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ uṇhodakena nhāpetvā uṇhodakena phāṇitaṁ āloletvā bhagavato pādāsi.
root_text.sn7.13:6.1: Atha kho devahito brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn7.13:6.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.sn7.13:6.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho devahito brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn7.13:10.1: Evaṁ vutte, devahito brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.13:10.3: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn7.13:1.2: Now at that time the Buddha was afflicted by winds.
translation_text.sn7.13:1.4: Then the Buddha said to Upavāṇa,
translation_text.sn7.13:1.5: “Go on then, Upavāṇa, find some hot water for me.”
translation_text.sn7.13:1.6: “Yes, sir,” replied Upavāṇa. He robed up, and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the home of the brahmin Devahita, and stood silently to one side.
translation_text.sn7.13:2.2: shaven, wrapped in his outer robe.
translation_text.sn7.13:2.3: What do you want? What are you looking for?
translation_text.sn7.13:2.4: What have you come here to ask for?”
translation_text.sn7.13:3.1: “The perfected one, the Holy One in the world,
translation_text.sn7.13:3.2: the sage is afflicted by winds.
translation_text.sn7.13:3.4: give it to the sage, brahmin.
translation_text.sn7.13:4.1: I wish to bring it to the one
translation_text.sn7.13:4.2: who is esteemed by the estimable,
translation_text.sn7.13:5.1: Then Devahita had a man fetch a carrying-pole with hot water. He also presented Upavāṇa with a jar of molasses.
translation_text.sn7.13:5.2: Then Venerable Upavāṇa went up to the Buddha and bathed him with the hot water. Then he stirred molasses into hot water and presented it to the Buddha.
translation_text.sn7.13:5.3: Then the Buddha’s illness died down.
translation_text.sn7.13:6.1: Then the brahmin Devahita went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn7.13:6.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side,
translation_text.sn7.13:6.3: and addressed the Buddha in verse:
translation_text.sn7.13:7.1: “Where should you give an available gift?
translation_text.sn7.13:7.2: Where is a gift very fruitful?
translation_text.sn7.13:7.3: How does one who is donating
translation_text.sn7.13:7.4: ensure the success of their religious donation?”
translation_text.sn7.13:8.2: sees heaven and places of loss,
translation_text.sn7.13:8.3: and has attained the ending of rebirth,
translation_text.sn7.13:8.4: is a sage of perfect insight.
translation_text.sn7.13:9.1: Here you should give an available gift;
translation_text.sn7.13:9.2: what’s given here is very fruitful.
translation_text.sn7.13:9.3: That’s how a sponsor of sacrifices
translation_text.sn7.13:9.4: ensures the success of their religious donation.”
translation_text.sn7.13:10.1: When he had spoken, the brahmin Devahita said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.13:10.3: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
